author: Q | Taitus | LMRUstur
content:
Good day, Founders,

Currently, contracts at the various space stations are selling at record speeds, getting snatched up faster each time. This is largely due to whales, who—with their massive manufacturing and transport capabilities—are able to quickly supply all stations, regardless of the random contract renewal intervals.

This situation is pushing smaller players out of the game. First, we don’t have the same production capacity; and second, even when we manage to identify where the contracts will appear, we don’t have enough time to move assets there before they’re gone.

For this reason, we propose that daily contract sales be limited based on each account’s XP. This measure would not only prevent whales from flooding the stations with contracts in minutes, but also help regulate the daily emission of Atlas, boost morale among smaller players, and promote a more balanced distribution—making it less likely for Atlas to be dumped onto the market.

We believe that switching to a biweekly contract drop won’t solve the issue. On the contrary, it would likely make contracts disappear within seconds of release, further excluding players with lower capacities.

Thank you for your attention and for your commitment to building a fairer and more sustainable in-game economy.

author: [AEP] Lerinor [STAR]
content: I can appreciate your frustration here, my friend. And it certainly is not our goal to drive users out of the game, particularly due to economic inequality or lack of opportunity. With that said, these FICs are not intended to be an infinitely available economic flow. They are one of several opportunities present in the game at the moment. It's unlikely we will allocate any time implementing restrictions on claiming beyond the current mechanics (which focus primarily on mitigating emissions vs. individual player restrictions), particularly given our focus on C4 and proximity to release of that new feature set. There are a number of alternative economic routes available, and I would suggest assessing if any of those other opportunities are more readily available and consistent for you and your particular circumstance.

author: 《VΛ》redcali
content: The map is closer to Stellaris style, not because of tech limitations but UI. Strategy games are quite difficult in 3D. Homeworld and nebulous fleet command did it in tactical battles which could be very interesting to introduce but at a galaxy wide level adding 3d would add a lot of cognitive complexity.   From a realism lens we're also only using a small fraction of the systems in the galaxy, the milkyway has ~100 billion stars where we're representing ~1000. Assuming even distribution of resources and habitable worlds it would be very unlikely for stars with top 0.00...001% richness to be on top of each other. (Assuming the stars we show are the ones with rich resources worth spending time to mine)  At a tactical or in-system level though 3d makes a lot more sense. Planets tend to be near a system plane (spin of the star when it was forming the system) but often perturb off it, especially far out planets.

author: [SAG] Swift42 [CODE]
content:
Something is wrong with the crafting timestamp / starbase time.
I wondered why SLYA throws ix errors with the message "The crafting process has not completed"
It is because an unfinished job reports e.g. these times:
endtime: 1736320775 
starbase time: 1736325459.685
but because endtime is smaller than the starbase time, SLYA thinks the job is already done.
So either something is wrong on your side or some values fundamentally changed.

author: [SAG] Swift42 [CODE]
content: According to my findings, there are two main problems: The speed isn't correct, it's always the reduced crafting speed (according to the SB level). And on top of it Sage displays the end time wrong AND the timer runs too slow. So e.g. a job ends in 15 minutes, Sage displays 2 hours, but the timer will run for 6 hours (e.g. on ONI-5, where the speed is 27.5%). (a fixed version of SLYA that circumvents the bug runs locally on my system, but I'll wait for the fix in Sage before publishing a new SLYA version)

author: [HEIM] SDU pimpUstur
content:
A question for the team:

I have been wondering, for quite some time now, how the game would operate once combat is online at the same time as being able to have 100% visibility of any account you want at any time anywhere in Galia. Normally, in games of this nature, there are elements of obfuscation that make things like combat and permanent destruction an activity that can be engaged with by anyone willing and daring enough to do so.,
My concern, (and would like to get the teams opinion on this), is that with being able to see everything on the map with zero obfuscation, that this will favor only people who have buttloads of fleets (and know they will never get contested), and will leave no room for things like tact and skill for smaller groups of players who might want to be looking to exploit the obvious advantages that bigger players and guilds might have.,
Not to mention, that with everything being visible all the time, this would favor always the attacker over the defender (which seems counter-intuitive for games of this nature) usually the defender is the one that should gain some form of an advantage.,
Is there going to be any form of strategy with combat ? or is it literally going to be "bigger group of ships win" all the time. Will defenders get some kind of advantage over attackers, in terms of combat, which is generally the case in games of this nature ?,

author: ROME|King Bryan-Titan Analytics [STAR]
content: Update on this. Both pros and cons. As we are dynamically solving for the food upkeep update, we discovered that we are not able to simply deposit food to any SB on the fly, administratively. Non dilutive to LP, and the 1f/s rate is exceptionally low, so food deposits go a long way

author: Definitely_Not_JayWalk
content:
@SW4GN3R Super curious about something I have been thinking about regarding onboarding of new players.  Is it by direction that mods do not EVER mention BASED to new players inquiring about whenther the game is ready (unless they have specific questons about it after finding it on their own)?  Even then mods tend to push people away by saying that they won't earn unless they have big fleets.  I find it odd that with BASED being the heart of SA and the main aspect of the P2E aspect that it has never been pushed.  Every time a new potential player comes in asking if the game is ready most mods just send a key and a UE5 link or 2 usually without saying that it's just a demo or any explanation.  Then almost always the player realizes that there isn't much to do and then leaves.  Is this by direction?  Just seems odd that the one aspect that could help the the economy moving gets literally ZERO attention or promotion.
Seems to me a SUPER easy way to fix this would be a standard "pre-generated" statement (and/or link) that shows a simple overview of both with a line or two regarding the future direction.  The fact that we STILL can't answer the question of whether this game is ready to play is a HUGE onboarding problem.

author: Definitely_Not_JayWalk
content: No, it's not by direction. We say things based on our experience with the onboarding process so far. We had too many frustrated players leaving because they realised after purchasing an airbike and crafting for two days that transaction fees eat them up. We recommend doing proper research before diving in, and manage expectations by telling them that without a certain amount invested they shouldn't expect the world. Not sure where the accusation comes from that we never ever mentioned it, but feel free to elaborate further in ⁠💥┃fr-chat  My personal approach to this changed with fleet rentals avaliable, can't speak for the other mods.

author: odvb
content:
@Definitely_Not_JayWalk Just wanted to drop a quick note on the earlier reply I gave about whether the game is ready to play.
Looking back, I probably could’ve explained things more clearly instead of just linking the key and UE5 launcher. At the moment, I thought the best way to show that the game is live was to point straight to the current UE5 build — especially since some new players are more visual and want to jump right in. He also is sort of a veteran and here since 2021.
But I fully recognize that every newcomer has different expectations and knowledge levels — and we don’t always know how familiar they are with SAGE, BASED, or the broader roadmap. It’s something I (and probably all of us mods) are constantly working on improving: balancing quick responses with the right context.
I really appreciate the feedback that’s been brought up — we all want to improve onboarding and give people the best possible first impression of Star Atlas. If anyone has ideas for a better “starter response” or wants to collaborate on one, I’m 100% down.

author: SW4GN3R [STAR]
content: I can chime in here.   Mods have been helping onboard new players for years now. They have even spent hours explaining to new users how to get into SAGE on 1:1 calls. They tell people about our live products, but we have seen way greater success getting new players engaged in Unreal Engine 5 first and then bridging them to SAGE. In fact, that's basically our funnel.  We have seen many new users drop because they feel super overwhelmed when we try to get them into SAGE as the first thing they experience with Star Atlas, so we usually prefer the journey to start from what's most attractive for new eyes like the Unreal Engine 5 game, trailers, and then walk them through the whole funnel into reaching SAGE.  We will have major improvements to SAGE onboarding with Holosim, and also after we release C4. Our engineering team hasn't had time to work on onboarding flows or user journey from a product side, which is KEY to retaining and walking new eyes through the game.   Besides that and more to your point, we have a full guide that starts on ⁠❓┃star-atlas-game⁠, which walks new users through everything they need to know to get started, including how to play SAGE. And we have it in the user flow for new joins that they need to read at least the first channel before being able to interact in the Discord.   This said, appreciate your feedback, and we are always down to improve our onboarding flows based on what the community says. Also, know that you can reach our mods and community team directly to discuss improvements on this flow before needing to tag the CEO

author: [AEP] Mandalorian (Djin Djarin)
content:
Hey Team,

I had a blast playing the game over the past two days—it’s incredibly engaging and already has that addictive quality! Great work on the progress so far. Below are some observations from my sessions, offered as constructive feedback to help refine the experience:
Hit Registration on Moving Targets: I noticed hit registration feels slightly off when tracking moving targets. Is this due to a hitscan system where leading shots isn’t required, or could it be a tracking issue? Clarification or tweaks here might improve the shooting experience.,Stability Issues: As you mentioned, there were server crashes, and I also experienced game client crashes. It’s unclear if these are related?,Vaulting Mechanics: Vaulting feels a bit clunky at times. For example, when initiating a vault and pressing space to transition into flight mode at the peak, my character resets to the ground and restarts the vault. Smoother transitions between actions could improve fluidity.,Graphics and DLSS Settings: Switching DLSS between Performance and Quality modes caused some odd visual effects. Additionally, adjusting certain settings made the graphics look off, but reverting to default presets fixed it. Performance was solid at 115 FPS on Epic settings—great job there!,Third-Person Camera and FOV: In third-person mode, the camera sometimes gets obstructed by my character, blocking visibility of enemies ahead. Increasing the third-person FOV (perhaps up to 120) could help maintain situational awareness.,Third-Person Aiming Sensitivity: Aiming in third-person feels noticeably slower compared to the standard view. Adding a separate sensitivity adjustment for third-person aiming could allow for better customization and consistency.,Summary: The game is shaping up amazingly, and I’m thrilled to be part of this project! Your team has done fantastic work, and I hope this feedback comes across as constructive to help polish the experience further. Let me know if you need more details on any of these points!
Best regards,
Mando

reply.preview: Hey Team, I had a blast playing the game over the past two days—it’s incredibly engaging and already has that addictive quality! Great work on the progress so far. Below are some observations from my sessions, offered as constructive feedback to help refine the experience: Hit Registration on Moving Targets: I noticed hit registration feels slightly off when tracking moving targets. Is this due to a hitscan system where leading shots isn’t required, or could it be a tracking issue? Clarification or tweaks here might improve the shooting experience. , Stability Issues: As you mentioned, there were server crashes, and I also experienced game client crashes. It’s unclear if these are related? , Vaulting Mechanics: Vaulting feels a bit clunky at times. For example, when initiating a vault and pressing space to transition into flight mode at the peak, my character resets to the ground and restarts the vault. Smoother transitions between actions could improve fluidity. , Graphics and DLSS Settings: Switching DLSS between Performance and Quality modes caused some odd visual effects. Additionally, adjusting certain settings made the graphics look off, but reverting to default presets fixed it. Performance was solid at 115 FPS on Epic settings—great job there! , Third-Person Camera and FOV: In third-person mode, the camera sometimes gets obstructed by my character, blocking visibility of enemies ahead. Increasing the third-person FOV (perhaps up to 120) could help maintain situational awareness. , Third-Person Aiming Sensitivity: Aiming in third-person feels noticeably slower compared to the standard view. Adding a separate sensitivity adjustment for third-person aiming could allow for better customization and consistency. , Summary: The game is shaping up amazingly, and I’m thrilled to be part of this project! Your team has done fantastic work, and I hope this feedback comes across as constructive to help polish the experience further. Let me know if you need more details on any of these points! Best regards, Mando
